### Where permission is granted E+W


25.8. — (1) In any proceedings, where the court grants permission as mentioned in [ F17 section 13(1), (3) or (5) of the 2014 Act or ] rule 25.4 [ F17 (2) ] —


(2) In children proceedings, in addition to the directions in paragraph (1)(b), the court will give directions—


Textual Amendments


F17 Words in rule 25.8(1) inserted (22.4.2014) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 3) Rules 2014 (S.I. 2014/843) , rules 1 , 50


### General requirement for expert evidence to be given in a written report E+W


25.9. — (1) Expert evidence is to be given in a written report unless the court directs otherwise.


(2) The court will not direct an expert to attend a hearing unless it is necessary to do so in the interests of justice.


### Written questions to experts E+W


25.10. — (1) A party may put written questions about an expert’s report to—


(2) Unless the court directs otherwise or a practice direction provides otherwise, written questions under paragraph (1)—


(3) An expert’s answers to questions put in accordance with paragraph (1)—


(4) Where—


the court may make one or both of the following orders in relation to the party who instructed the expert—


### Court’s power to direct that evidence is to be given by a single joint expert E+W


25.11. — (1) Where two or more parties wish to put expert evidence before the court on a particular issue, the court may direct that the evidence on that issue is to be given by a single joint expert.
